problem_statement: How vibe coding reconfigures product-team workflows and raises oversight challenges, as AI-generated code is unreliable and prone to over-reliance yet still requires human debugging, verification, and accountability before reaching production.
magnitude_evidence: recent studies show accepting <44% of AI-generated code [Becker et al. 2025]; based on interviews with 22 product team members
insufficiency_evidence: "There's no firewall, nothing to stop bad AI code before it hits the system."[P17]; the Replit case where AI-generated changes reached production with minimal review; "I can see the UI is all right...it hasn't been through any real security checks or code reviews."[P15]
solution_mechanisms: four-stage human-in-loop workflow (ideation, generation, debugging, review)
expert oversight to remove harmful/redundant code
treat AI output as provisional and verify
version control / traceable AI action histories
automatic verification layers ('firewall') for AI code
